medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Ms. ___ is a ___ year old female with history of multiple sclerosis, bilateral recurrent DVTs, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, osteoarthritis, and dementia who presents with severe abdominal pain, nausea, and emesis. The pain started 3 hours prior to their arrival. In the ED, she denies chest pain, fevers, chills, or other symptoms.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Multiple sclerosis - relapsing remitting disease; Bilateral DVT's post TKR; Diabetes; HTN; Night time urinary incontinence.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital Signs: T 98.4, BP 178/95, HR 104, RR 20, O2 100 on RA. General: Alert, able to follow simple commands and respond to simple questions, oriented to being in a hospital but could not name which.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Able to move all extremities with purpose; Clear bilateral lung exam; Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 + S2, no murmurs, rubs, gallops; Abdomen soft, tender possibly lower > upper quadrants.

laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Potassium decreased from 9.0 to 3.8, Hemoglobin decreased from 9.9 to 9.5

findings.Hepatic Masses: New hypodense enhancing hepatic masses mostly in the left lobe
findings.Thickened urinary bladder wall: Thickened urinary bladder wall seen with cystitis
findings.Other Findings: No small bowel obstruction or diverticulitis

findings.New foci: Punctate new foci of restricted diffusion in the right frontal and parietal lobes

findings.Waves: Q waves III and TWI III

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Abdominal pain UTI, suspected cancer of the biliary tract
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with severe abdominal pain, and initial imaging indicated concerning findings of multiple hepatic lesions which suggested a potential malignancy such as cholangiocarcinoma, as well as cystitis. The combination of the patient's symptoms, lab findings, and imaging were critical in directing towards a diagnosis of possible cancer.
